headline: Median MP is 52, citizen 31. Parliament refused PM term limits by two votes.
context: Median Malaysian MP age is 52.4; median citizen is 31. Only 11 of 222 MPs are aged 35 or under. On 3 March 2026 Parliament rejected a 10-year PM term limit — two votes short of the two-thirds bar.

cards.big: Median Malaysian MP is 52. Median citizen is 31. Only 5% of MPs are under 35 — and the constitution is not what's blocking renewal.
cards.sub: Half the voters are under 40. Two months ago Parliament rejected a 10-year PM term limit — by two votes. Zero MPs aged 30 or younger are women.

cards.big: Same cast since the 1970s. Lim Kit Siang in DAP for 56 years. Anwar in UMNO since 1982. Najib was MP for 46 years until his SRC conviction.
cards.sub: Hadi Awang, 78, has led PAS for 24 years — yet in Sept 2025 he himself backed PM-under-70. Sabah and Sarawak: 25% of seats, less than MA63's third.

cards.big: Ministers may qualify for gratuities reported up to RM2 million. Stacked pensions across MP, ADUN, and cabinet roles can exceed RM100,000 a month.
cards.sub: IDEAS counted 238 political appointments to government boards under the current government in 35 months. PN era: 53 in 17 months; PH: 25 in 22.

cards.big: France's 34-year-old PM in 2024 was replaced within months by 73-year-olds. Anwar's Dec 2025 'reset' made the Speaker's son Youth Minister at 29.
cards.sub: Indonesia's MPs are 4.5% aged 30 or younger — close to Malaysia's 5%. Women hold 13.5% of seats; only one Orang Asli has ever been an elected MP.

cards.big: The candidacy age has been 18 since 2019. Two months ago Parliament rejected a 10-year PM term limit — by two votes. The machinery refuses to constrain itself.
Imagine a relay race that pays each runner more the longer they hold the baton — and the baton tends to pass to the runner's son.
cards.sub: Up to RM2 million gratuity per ministerial exit. Up to RM100,000 a month in stacked pensions.

cards.big: The bottleneck is not age — it's the machinery that also produces 13.5% women MPs, one Orang Asli MP ever, and zero women aged 30 or younger.
cards.sub: Voters wanted renewal twice: unanimous in 2019, two votes short in March 2026. The question at GE16: who names the machinery and runs against it?

_provenance.compression_notes: Stage 6 v2 cards totaled 274-1002 chars; compressed to 274-300 chars each to fit reader format. View card reduced from 1002→290 chars by extracting the WhatsApp-quote sentence as big and the actionable question as sub.
_provenance.opinion_shift_rationale: 68 (Significant tier). Headline alone gives WHAT (the gap and the rejected reform) but cards add WHY (machinery, pensions, GLC patronage), HOW (dynasty cycle — Speaker's son as Youth Minister; 238 IDEAS appointments), and SCOPE (multi-axis exclusion: women, Orang Asli, East Malaysia). Reader who only sees headline misses the structural diagnosis and the multi-axis inclusion gap.
_provenance.final_score_calculation: 4-stage arithmetic mean (pa 84 + ba 55 + fc 68 + sr 82) / 4 = 72.25. Stages 4 and 5 are retired per CLAUDE.md, so af and ct are not included in the published finalScore even though they were used to inform synthesis.
